steps.title: Explain the underground traps
steps.content: N. pudica has prey-catching pitchers underground, unlike other carnivorous plants. The researchers found it on dry ridges whose surfaces host few plants and animals, so the unusual trap location points to an adaptive advantage below the surface.

steps.title: Reject unsupported hypotheses
steps.content: Choice A predicts other undiscovered species. Choice B treats pitchers as moisture absorbers rather than prey traps. Choice C says animals bury the plant, which the text does not suggest. The answer is D.
